question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@DurationMin(seconds=1L)` constraint on `statusFrequency` to set it to exactly 1 second, causing the `SubscribeMetrics.status()` scheduler to fire every second and generate excessive log output that buries staking reward theft alerts in log noise?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `RestSubscriberProperties` with `limit=1` (via `AbstractSubscriberProperties`) to cause the REST subscriber to stop after verifying only one staking reward transaction, leaving all subsequent reward payouts unverified and enabling theft of unpaid rewards after the first transaction?
